programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Four hippos take a huge step into their new home, while Ben Fogle and Kate Humble revisit the unforgettable story of beloved tiger Kadu

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: James Broad and Lauren Wood help dog groomer Tanya to let go of her parents' treasured items to fund a long-dreamed-of trip to India, honouring her father's memory and reconnecting with her heritage

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don keeps the garden thriving through summer by cutting back perennials, managing long grass, pruning apples and sowing vegetables, before harvesting new potatoes and sweet peas. Adam Frost visits a remarkable Surrey walled garden, while Sue Kent shares creative growing ideas, and experts explore healthier crops and beautiful crassulas

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire test their general knowledge skills in the hope of winning a prize

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Free-range pig farming couple SJ and Nat, along with organic cattle farmers Emma and Ian, try to sell their home-grown food at the Lancashire Game and Country Festival

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Transforming a casual dining concept into a chain of restaurants is one of the most high-risk ventures in hospitality. The chef goes behind the scenes at two restaurants looking to expand their businesses. They include Harvest, a new fusion restaurant right next to Windsor Castle. Tom also shares his own plans regarding The Butcher's Tap and Grill

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: The animal families face the formidable monsoon rains and the giant otter cubs must watch out for predatory jaguars and learn to swim before their river floods. The coatis have to eat as much as they can ahead of their habitat disappearing underwater, while the youngest capuchin monkey needs to master some clever survival skills. Stephen Mangan narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Norwich Cathedral, once home to Benedictine monks. Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher pick out some items to take to auction. Among them are an old astronomical map and a watercolour by a local artist. Paul also explores the history of the nearby Broads

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Derbyshire with a recently married couple who are making an epic location shift and swapping the Blue Mountains in Australia for the wilds of the Peak District. They have a significant budget of £750,000 and have their hearts set on a character home with a large garden for their three dogs. Away from his quest, Jules comes across an unlikely centre of manufacturing nestled in the county's rural countryside

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts joins archaeologists on digs of northern Britain, revealing a Roman Emperor's lost bath house, the sunken treasures of medieval pilgrims and a formidable fortress perched on top of a Scottish mountain

programme.title: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke
programme.desc: When popular vlogger Ruby Franke was arrested for child abuse in August 2023, it quickly became one of the biggest trending stories in America. For years, the Frankes' YouTube channel had documented a wholesome, happy family life, with nearly three million subscribers tuning in at its peak. But happy families are rarely what they seem. As the cracks began to show, the family turned to life coach Jodi Hildebrandt for guidance - and rapidly found themselves trapped in a nightmare

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Lawrence arranges a special reunion on a boat for his old university friends Darren and Callum, but the day does not unfold quite as they expected. As they strike out across the remote lake, their conversation takes them into troubled waters. Darkly comic anthology, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Mark Gatiss and Diane Morgan

programme.title: The North York Moors: A Wild Year
programme.desc: The red grouse of the North York Moors, which enjoy the shoots of the heather in spring, which is almost all that can grow on the sandstone of the moorlands

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events with Faisal Islam

programme.title: The Inspection
programme.desc: A gay black man enlists in the Marine Corps and ends up at boot camp on Parris Island, South Carolina. He initially meets the physical requirements, but is not as successful in disguising his sexual orientation, making him the target of a near-lethal hazing from training instructor Leland Laws and a fellow recruit. LGBTQ+ drama, starring Jeremy Pope, Gabrielle Union and Bokeem Woodbine

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Central Leeds crew Nagina and Mo are called to a siege where police are currently negotiating with a man who is threatening to harm himself and the officers attending. They must be ready to enter the building to treat any potential casualties. Meanwhile, married crewmates Jen and Elliott are dispatched to a 61-year-old in Yeadon, who has had a suspected stroke, then treat a patient having a seizure at the train station

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: Chris Packham tells the story of how life on Earth has developed over more than four billion years, charting the changes that gave rise to five modern animals, beginning with the African elephant

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Two young Amur tigers prepare to leave home as part of an international breeding programme to help secure the future of one of the world's rarest big cats. Ben Fogle and Kate Humble revisit an extraordinary conservation adventure when Longleat keepers travelled to Tanzania to help conservationist Tony Fitzjohn relocate endangered African wild dogs

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: A young family hope their 1980s classic toy will click with the dealers, an intriguing seat leads to a guessing game, and Nigel Havers is enchanted by a piece of London history

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus is on a mission to discover the French equivalent of British fish and chips, while uncovering the secrets of the versatile Provençal olive

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: The journalist in conversation with people who influence our political thinking about what has shaped theirs

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire test their general knowledge skills in the hope of winning a prize, with the week's overall winner being decided

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Alpaca farmers Debbie and Paul, along with fellow alpaca farmer Bev, hope to impress the judges as they compete at the Westmorland County Show in Cumbria

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The chef looks at how the industry delivers exceptional service on a huge scale. In Wolverhampton, Tom meets the team behind Grand Station, a large events venue housed in a former railway station. And the presenter has a huge catering event of his own - the Six Nations rugby match between England and France at Twickenham

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: A fierce drought culminates in huge and ferocious fires, and the capuchin monkeys, giant otters and coatis must use their survival skills to endure the extreme conditions. The animals are also looking for mates so they can breed in time to ensure the next generation are born at the optimal moment

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and David Fletcher are at Norwich Cathedral to select items to take to auction, including a Masonic watch and a sought after commemorative mug by Eric Ravilious. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the former home of Robert Walpole Britain's first prime minister

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay compete on double points Friday before the winner takes home the trophy

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp suggests a 'reverse-moves' approach to exercise, including walking downhill instead of up and lowering weights instead of lifting them, both of which could have significant health benefits

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Landing at Glenuig, Paul Murton makes his way inland to Loch Moidart and MacDonald country, walking the old coffin road, which was used for centuries by funeral parties, taking their dead to a sacred isle. On the track Paul meets a trials bike enthusiast and learns the secrets of a 'dabbing coat' before landing off the Green Isle, where he rings a 1,100-year-old bell for the dead. Taking a boat north, Paul sails up Loch Shiel to Glenfinnan where Bonnie Prince Charlie officially began his rising to reclaim the throne for the Stuart Dynasty. From a forgotten monument that commemorates this event, Paul heads back to the coast and Loch Ailort, where he learns how to knife fight before taking a trip across the loch to a deserted community on the roadless Ardnish peninsula

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham and Kirsty Wilson work on the garden's borders, replanting, filling gaps and replacing losses all around the plot. Kirsty will also be planting in the border behind Beechgrove's main pond, where other gaps have appeared, and she is building a new structure to create a habitat for pollinators

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Arit Anderson, Jamie Butterworth and Frances Tophill explore the first RHS Sandringham Flower Show, showcasing stunning gardens, floral displays and expert horticultural advice. Highlights include rose-growing tips, drought-tolerant achilleas, succulent care from enthusiastic collectors, and a Hampshire plantsman sharing his family's enduring nursery tradition and passion for gardening

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Modern re-imagining of the iconic Jersey-set crime drama, starring Damien Molony as troubled detective Jim Bergerac. When a member of one of the region's wealthiest families is murdered, the police want their best detective on the case. Meanwhile, a man from Bergerac's past makes a shocking accusation. With Zoe Wanamaker and Philip Glenister

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: The police are desperate to find a kidnapped baby before it is too late. Bergerac questions a convicted kidnapper, and the battle lines are drawn between these two old foes. Meanwhile, the detective also disappoints his daughter Kim again and incurs the wrath of his mother-in-law

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events with Matt Chorley

programme.title: Janet Planet
programme.desc: An 11-year-old girl drifts into a fantasy world as three visitors take an interest in her mother over the summer of 1991. But her vivid imagination begins to influence her real life. Coming-of-age drama, starring Zoe Ziegler, Julianne Nicholson, Will Patton and Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: There's a Gangster In My Flat - Panorama
programme.desc: Hazel Martin speaks to flat owners who paid reputable agencies to do tenancy checks but still ended up handing over their keys to criminals using fake IDs and references to take advantage of lax security in the rental market. Even when there appears to be clear evidence of criminal behaviour, gangs are escaping prosecution and landlords say it can take months to re-possess their properties

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie's training switches to care for the elderly, bringing back memories of her beloved dad. In college, the students get hands-on with different aspects of support and try out glasses that simulate conditions like glaucoma, along with weighted suits that affect movement and balance. Natalie's placement takes her to a Royal British Legion care home, where the focus is on keeping the residents active and involved. The programme also meets a group of men who gather as part of the Men's Shed community programme in Newtownabbey

programme.title: Starmer: What Went Wrong
programme.desc: The story of how Sir Keir Starmer's premiership unravelled, from Labour's historic 2024 landslide to the fatal miscalculations and internal revolt that forced him from power

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris find their holiday home double-booked by penguin arch-enemy Millie the Squid

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima and Big Lizard find some fascinatingly slippery fruit, but bringing it safely back to the ship is more challenging than they imagined

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: When Weasy keeps losing the obstacle race, he decides to cheat in an unusual way

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The Riddlemaster wears the Eye of Vernon hat to take over the forest, so the heroes head to Wood Elf Wood to get help

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings organised a huge party, leading to the cabin being invaded by bats

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: The duo must move a famous pop star's possessions from one mansion to another

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons: Rising Hope
programme.desc: Liko, Roy and Dot team up with Zirc and Onia on an unusual mission

programme.title: Winx Club - The Magic Is Back
programme.desc: The Winx plan a day of adventure in Magixia, but Griselda's restrictions and an incomplete project keep them groundeduntil Robin helps them sneak away

programme.title: The Next Step: Cheer
programme.desc: Kenzie struggles to finalise the team and Layla is anxious that she won't be on it, while Cammy helps Melody taunt their rivals

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Joel takes on an epic superhero challenge as he attempts to climb one of the UK's tallest buildings

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Arit Anderson, Jamie Butterworth and Frances Tophill explore the first RHS Sandringham Flower Show, showcasing stunning gardens, floral displays and expert horticultural advice. Highlights include rose-growing tips, drought-tolerant achilleas, succulent care from enthusiastic collectors, and a Hampshire plantsman sharing his family's enduring nursery tradition and passion for gardening

programme.title: This Wild Life
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Saba Douglas-Hamilton and the elephant rescue team race to save the life of a recently orphaned calf. The baby elephant is flown to an elephant orphanage in Nairobi, but with a terror threat on the Kenya's East coast, the family's safari camp is also in crisis. Saba must work to save the business and do all she can to protect Samburu's trusting elephants

programme.title: Funeral in Berlin
programme.desc: Former crook and disreputable spy Harry Palmer has to carefully engineer the defection of a Russian officer responsible for safeguarding vital military secrets - but the mission proves less than straightforward. Cold War thriller and the second film to feature Len Deighton's character, starring Michael Caine, Eva Renzi, Oscar Homolka, Paul Hubschmid and Guy Doleman

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young put the sewers through their paces with another three challenges themed around colours and prints, including an adaptive shell top involving a couple of tricky pattern matches. Hosted by Sophie Willan

programme.title: High Society
programme.desc: Romantic complications arise when a playboy composer sets out to win back his ex-wife. However, she is about to marry another man - and the situation becomes even more difficult when a cynical reporter enters the fray. Musical comedy based on The Philadelphia Story with a score by Cole Porter, starring Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Grace Kelly, with Louis Armstrong appearing as himself

programme.title: Casablanca
programme.desc: The American expat owner of a Moroccan nightclub is reunited with his old flame during the Second World War. Now married to a Czech freedom fighter, she needs his help to get them safely out of the country before they are captured by the Nazis - but the former lovers also have to wrestle the temptation to rekindle their simmering romance. Drama, with Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Claude Rains and Paul Henreid

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts James Lewis and Mark Stacey value antiques at Guildford Cathedral, including a collection of Doulton dogs, a mahogany window seat and an unusual ring. Presenter Paul Martin looks at the role local people played in the the venue's construction

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter Q, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Cumbria, visiting Levens Hall where she lends a hand planting spring bulbs, and hears some personal memories of a Lakeland lad. She then travels to Holehird to meet the volunteers who have restored a 19th-century garden to its former glory, before discovering the spur of Beatrix Potter's writing career

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani continues her east coast road trip, visiting the Naze, a nature reserve on the Essex coast. She also joins a group of fossil hunters on the search for the secrets revealed by coastal erosion. Plus, reports from the archive exploring the hidden histories of the British landscape

programme.title: Best of Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway looks back on three walks that have changed people's lives for the better as he visits the Lourdes of Wales, sees how walking aids a cancer patient's recuperation and helps a woman with sight-loss enjoy birdwatching

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham and Kirsty Wilson work on the garden's borders, replanting, filling gaps and replacing losses all around the plot. Kirsty will also be planting in the border behind Beechgrove's main pond, where other gaps have appeared, and she is building a new structure to create a habitat for pollinators

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt celebrates the best of seasonal produce, with recipes from Hasan Semay, Freddy Bird and Anna Haugh. Plus, archive clips of Rick Stein and the Hairy Bikers

programme.title: Indian Food Made Easy
programme.desc: Food writer and chef Anjum Anand travels around Britain, introducing people to a selection of ingredients from the subcontinent and showing how they can be combined to prepare dishes ranging from samosas and nan bread to traditional lamb curry and tandoori mushroom wraps. Anjum begins at Sherborne County Fair, where she attempts to persuade the masses to eat more healthily

programme.title: Indian Food Made Easy
programme.desc: Food writer and chef Anjum Anand prepares an Indian feast for a group of male friends to eat while watching the FA Cup final, with dishes including spicy lamb burgers with fresh chillies, ginger, mint and coriander, as well as oven-baked chilli chicken in breadcrumbs and paneer and vegetable kebabs

programme.title: Operation Crossbow
programme.desc: Three Allied engineers parachute into occupied Holland on a mission to infiltrate and destroy a German missile base. The trio team up with the local resistance, but two of the men are unaware the third is a Nazi agent. Second World War adventure, starring George Peppard, Sophia Loren, Trevor Howard, Tom Courtenay and John Mills

programme.title: Cricket: The Hundred
programme.desc: Trent Rockets Women v London Spirit Women (Start-time 2.30pm). Coverage of the match from Trent Bridge in Nottingham. The Spirit won the toss and elected to field in the match here last year, and went on to regret that decision as they lost by 33 runs, being dismissed for 116 as they chased the Rockets first innings total of 149/6

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams hunt for profitable antiques in Newark, Nottinghamshire, with experts Nick Hall and Izzie Balmer before auctioneer Charles Hanson oversees the final sale in Derbyshire. Presenter Eric Knowles visits a Nottinghamshire collector with a record-breaking collection of historic brewery trays

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Keepers bid farewell to wolf Terry as concern grows for his mate Meg and possible pups. There's a look back at Winky the wheeled tortoise, Kate Humble visits the hippos, and Megan McCubbin looks into rhino health checks and tongue tests

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Surrey man Bobby Williams brings in the powered wheelchair that opened up a whole new sporting career for him as a youngster, allowing him to play the fiercely competitive game of powerchair football. Now moving into coaching, he wants to pass the chair on to a new player - but it needs restoration. The other items are a speedway flag that tells a remarkable Glaswegian love story, meticulously handcrafted jewellery and a bagatelle board

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Yasmine Akram, Cariad Lloyd, Geoff Norcott and Dennis Taylor test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week with a winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers bike from north to south through Europe, searching for the best baking each country has to offer. They start their 5,000-mile trek in Norway, picking up tips and recipes from professionals and home bakers - including a 95-year-old who still makes waffles on her 65-year-old waffle iron. The duo also rustle up some of their own favourite Norwegian recipes - Scandinavian rye bread, cardamom and lemon cookies, and ham and cheese-filled pastries

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: East Sussex pig farmer Paul and Kent sheep farmer Hannah compete in front of the judges for prize rosettes at Edenbridge & Oxted Agricultural Show, with welcome help from their family and friends. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on the planet from a new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. Satellites follow an elephant family that is struggling through a drought, reveal previously unknown penguin colonies and discover mysterious ice rings that could put seal pups in danger

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams compete at Ardingly antiques fair, aided by Jonathan Pratt and Kate Bliss. In East Grinstead, Charlie Ross learns about pioneering treatments for Second World War airmen

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Young sea lion Rocky joins the colony, while the park celebrates 60 years since the UK's inaugural drive-through safari first opened its gates

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young put the sewers through their paces with another three challenges themed around colours and prints, including an adaptive shell top involving a couple of tricky pattern matches. Hosted by Sophie Willan

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Yasmine Akram, Cariad Lloyd, Geoff Norcott and Dennis Taylor go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The second leg of Si King and Dave Myers' journey in search of Europe's finest baking takes them through the Low Countries. The Netherlands' heritage as a spice-trading nation shines through in such dishes as Dutch apple pie smothered in cinnamon, and Jewish buns coated in muscovado sugar. In Belgium, the duo make their own chocolate truffle cheesecake, and get busy again in Luxembourg, cooking a pork pie with Riesling jelly. Along the way they snort chocolate, taste the sweet delights of Bruges and get a recipe for macaroons with ice-cream

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Friends Neil and Kai started their chilli farm in 2019 and now grow over 500 pesticide-free plants in their Wye Valley polytunnels. Here, they launch their new chilli ice-cream at Abergavenny Food Festival. Kevin and Natalie also get customers' taste buds tingling with their award-winning Welsh cider, perry and apple juice. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space reveal the stories behind Earth's most remarkable patterns and shapes, including freshwater lagoons in Brazil, mysterious light spots in the Australian outback, endless green canopy in the Congo rainforest, and the twists and turns of the Amazon river, which provides a home for rehabilitated manatees

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey value items and collectibles brought in by the public to Lulworth Castle in Dorset. Plus, presenter Paul Martin explores the turbulent history of the Weld family and the anti-Catholic persecution they were subject to for more than 300 years

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Karen Hauer, Storm Huntley, Joe Marler and Tom Read Wilson test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball prescribes ten minutes of sunshine a day for retired woodworker Neville, who has suffered a series of heart attacks. She explains how just ten minutes of sunlight a day has been shown to improve heart health, strengthen bones and lift mood

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Stories
programme.desc: In the Lake District, Rick meets Kevin Tickle, a chef who loves to forage, and the sheep farmer and author James Rebanks. He visits an old friend in Morecambe Bay with a secret recipe for the best potted shrimps. In Yorkshire, he discovers the story of yorkshire pudding and visits the UK's biggest tofu factory. He cooks tofu pad thai, aubergine biryani and, with his son Jack, makes his mum's shepherd's pie

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Rob Rinder and Monica Galetti work at Six Senses Ibiza, on the island's north coast. They help guests in the hotel's on-site clinic experience 'biohacking' therapies, from cryotherapy to heated infrared masks, promising to delay aging and increase vitality. Rob helps the hotel's 'energy balancing' expert to create a cacao ceremony to increase spiritual wellness. In the basement, Monica uncovers a recording studio with a party space for the music scene that the Island is best known for

programme.title: The Vampire Lestat: Interview with the Vampire
programme.desc: Gothic horror, starring Jacob Anderson. The switch from recluse to rock star begins Lestat's tell-all recordings, leading to a brutal hotel confrontation. However, global turmoil lies ahead

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up Jamali Maddix comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by Fern Brady and Sindhu Vee

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: The New Boy
programme.desc: Australian drama following an Aboriginal orphan boy as he arrives at a remote monastery. Starring Cate Blanchett, Aswan Reid, Deborah Mailman and Wayne Blair

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie learns about dementia care, which proves to be one of the most emotional weeks of her course so far. She takes part in an immersive experience designed to disorientate and confuse - giving a sense of what it's like to have the disease - and talks to her agent Scott, who was married to her former EastEnders colleague Barbara Windsor, and how he looked after her as the disease took over. Finally she puts her learning into practice on a special placement at the Royal British Legion's Poppy Lodge dementia unit

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: The police are desperate to find a kidnapped baby before it is too late. Bergerac questions a convicted kidnapper, and the battle lines are drawn between these two old foes. Meanwhile, the detective also disappoints his daughter Kim again and incurs the wrath of his mother-in-law

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Susan Calman, Max Fosh and Noreen Khan on their knowledge of Bamburgh in Northumberland

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore presents the show where experts transform and then sell items about to be dumped at a tip. Chantelle and some new friends get up extra early to see if they can spot some little owls in the park, whilst Rory meets a bird expert who is tagging more little baby owls. Chantelle and Rory's fact files help answer all of Carlson's questions. With the help of the Teeny Tiny Creatures poem we discover taht, like lots of other teeny tiny creatures, little owls are superheroes and help our planet in their own special way. programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Dan the border collie is a search and rescue dog who works with his owner Jamie. His super nose follows scents and smells like clues so they can rescue people who need their help. Dan is busy digging a hole when his owner Jamie gets a very important phone call - police officer Penny needs them to go to Woofton Woods to look for a lost birdwatcher who is out trying to spot a rare bird, the Woofton woodpecker. After locating the birdwatcher's car, Dan has to find the scent to discover which of the many paths she has taken through the woods. But the scent takes them off the path and into the canopy of trees, which are full of low-hanging branches and logs. Jamie kits Dan up with his breeze-busting bell so that he can hear him in the thicker forest. But after tracking down the lost birdwatcher, Dan and Jamie head back to the car park only to discover her car is still there. The birdwatcher has gone missing again! Can Dan and Jamie complete a double rescue?

Sylvie and her owner Katrina are helping a girl called Akilah, who is joining the local school after arriving from a foreign country. Lots of things will be very different for her, so Sylvie will need her super heart to make her feel as welcome as possible. After meeting Akilah and her mum, along with Fred the teaching assistant, Sylvie takes her to the school hall for some lunch, stopping to admire some artwork along the way. Then Sylvie acts as Akilah's furry tour guide on a whistlestop tour of the school, including a trip to the library, before guiding her to her new classroom to meet the teacher and all her new friends. There will be lots of new faces, and everyone will be talking in a language she's still learning - but with Sylvie and her super heart by her side, Akilah is sure to make lots of friends.

Charli is meeting her friend Annabel for lunch at the local café. They haven't been out for a lunch treat for ages, and the café's speciality is Tinks's favourite - chicken. First, Charli parks her car using her blue parking badge and then gets her wheelchair out of the boot using a hoist. programme.desc: Stewie falls in love with an old friend, but is unhappy to discover she does not share his feelings. Meanwhile, Lois makes Peter watch 'chick flicks', inspiring him to make a film of his own. With the voice of Drew Barrymore

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The constabulary are on guard as the village is plagued by a series of mysterious explosions, while a redundant church provides Vernon and David with their next fortune-hunting endeavour. Nostalgic drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Crime drama starring Brenda Blethyn. The detective investigates a fire that rips through a holiday park on the Northumberland coast, destroying three cabins. A young woman's body is discovered and the resort's owner, Jim Viner, fears the victim is his sister Deena when he returns from a weekend away with his daughter Clare. With Jim's suspicions confirmed, a police report then reveals Clare's boyfriend Ryan and his friends had been partying near the scene hours before the blaze started, but where are they now, and where is Deena's best friend Sally? Co-starring Kenny Doughty, Cush Jumbo and Jon Morrison

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A heartbroken Sidney agrees to help parishioner Isabel prepare for her wedding to Arthur, the new man in town, but the bride's stubborn mother, Daisy, proves to be a big impediment to the occasion as she believes the groom is out to kill her. When Daisy is then found dead, Mrs Maguire calls in Geordie to investigate, and Sidney finds himself dragged into the case - despite vowing to leave police business behind. While the victim seems to have died of natural causes, Arthur and Isabel are pushing for a fast cremation, suggesting the couple have something to hide. Jean Marsh and Isla Blair guest star

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Sidney is woken in the early hours by a fire in the village and, putting his life on the line, races to rescue Marion Taylor from the burning house, much to the relief of her husband Dominic. But there are questions surrounding the blaze - was it really an accident, and why was Marion so reluctant to leave? Normally Sidney would turn to Geordie to help solve a mystery like this, but he finds the detective is far from his usual self. When Dominic is then found stabbed to death, it's Geordie's junior, Atkins, who is sent to head up the case - and he's not exactly a fan of interfering vicars

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: In a grim turn of events, the tooth of the abducted woman is found in the debris from a wood chipper, while Walker's ex-wife's boyfriend turns psychotic

programme.desc: The Ropers' bed collapses and George finds himself short of cash to acquire a new one. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

Feature-length episode of the crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Caroline Harker and Maurice Roeves

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An investigation into a cattle auctioneer puts Mike's career in danger when he finds himself under suspicion of taking bribes. The case also puts his relationship with his wife in jeopardy, culminating in a final, shocking revelation from Jackie. Meanwhile, Vernon attempts to bottle the secret of eternal youth

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A mischievous trickster finally gets his comeuppance when his body is found floating below the weir in Midsomer Deverell. The extent of his double life starts to emerge when three family friends and a long-lost niece produce wills claiming his fortune, and his collection of erotic photographs from the 1960s gives Barnaby and Jones the key to the tangled relationships and feuds blighting the family

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Sidney takes Geordie to Johnny Johnson's London jazz club, hoping to leave their worries in Grantchester for the night, but the backstage murder of Johnny's sister Claudette puts paid to that. When Archie, the victim's father and owner of the club, calls in a police officer pal to crack the case, Geordie immediately detects the copper is corrupt, while Sidney makes an important discovery about Claudette that she had kept hidden from all but those closest to her. As every lead seems to come back to Archie's violent past, recriminations within the family begin to play out

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: With Amanda and Guy's wedding fast approaching, and his betrayal of Hildegard still fresh in his mind, Sidney is thrown deep into crisis, and the murder of a local cop doesn't help matters as he allows himself to become drawn into another case with Geordie. However, tragedy strikes as the duo close in on the killer, and with Geordie's life hanging in the balance, the clergyman finds himself cut out of the investigation as the police close ranks. But that doesn't stop him enlisting the help of Mrs Maguire and Leonard in a daring mission to gather information on the man who shot his partner

programme.desc: A touring dance extravaganza comes to Midsomer, but nobody sees the vicious rivalries and long-running feuds that are brewing behind the scenes - at least not until they erupt into murder. Barnaby's investigation into the killing of a dancer is complicated by an unexpected visit from his father, while the sponsor of the event is at the centre of a conflict of his own as he tries to decide who should inherit his fortune. Guest starring Nigel Havers and Christopher Timothy, as well as former Strictly Come Dancing stars Cassidy Little, Faye Tozer, Danny Mac, Natalie Gumede and Tom Chambers

programme.desc: Maria asks Tom to help her get the truth about Liam's death. Janice starts her community service. The Platts are horrified to learn the identity of their new neighbours.

programme.desc: Maria questions Dev about the night of Liam's death, and Gail attempts to make peace with the Platts' new neighbours, while Tyrone takes steps to allay Molly's suspicions.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mike faces a stressful time as he struggles to cope with his wife's departure, and Tricia offers him a shoulder to cry on, but is beset by problems of her own after prescribing birth control to a 16-year-old. Meanwhile, Sergeant Craddock finds his efforts to prepare for an important visit hampered by a series of attacks on the station, and Vernon makes a startling discovery in his bathtub. Jason Durr and Clare Calbraith star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Part two of two. Carl Lomax's case comes to court, and a local reporter soon rakes up the dirt over Tricia's controversial decision to supply his girlfriend with birth-control drugs. Before long, he escapes from custody and goes on the run with Deborah. Meanwhile, Vernon invests in a new money-making scheme

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When the remains of a teenager are found half-buried in a wood, Vera finds herself investigating a 30-year-old mystery. Identified as Carrie Telling, a girl who disappeared during the 1984 miners' strike, the team retrieves news footage from the protests that shows her taking pictures in the background. It appears she got caught up in the unrest that day, contradicting her father's assertion that she went straight home. Photographs recovered from her camera yield further revelations for Vera. What did Carrie discover that ultimately led to her death?

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Detective drama set in the 1950s about a vicar and a police inspector solving crimes in rural Cambridgeshire. DC Phil Wilkinson arrests Sidney on a charge of sexual assault made by Harding Redmond after reading entries in the diary of his teenage daughter Abigail, who has now gone missing, along with the journal itself. After being grilled by DCI Benson, Sidney sets out to prove his innocence and is joined in his investigation by Geordie. James Norton and Robson Green star

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Geordie calls on Sidney for help as he investigates the apparent suicide of a Cambridge lecturer, who was found dead after falling from the spire of King's College Chapel. His wife and his star pupil, who used to partake in daredevil climbs of the college buildings alongside his mentor, are convinced he was too happy and content for it to be suicide. It appears to be a case of night climbing misadventure, until a student witness comes forward, leading Sidney and Geordie into a world of espionage and secrets. Detective drama, starring James Norton, Robson Green and Morven Christie

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part crime drama. Connor and Walker fear for the children's safety as their suspicions about Harwood are confirmed, and when they finally catch up with him it seems he has lost his mind. David Hayman, Victoria Smurfit and Charles Dance star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Museum of the Family is packed with visitors to see a collection of miniature doll's houses donated by a real estate mogul - only for the businessman to be shot dead in front of the crowd. Barnaby's investigation uncovers evidence of unsafe living conditions for the victim's tenants that would have given plenty of people a reason to kill - as well as a mysterious death that a man was imprisoned for, despite protesting his innocence.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon tells the police that Shane was blackmailing Ross about his affair with Donna, while Val warns Daz that she is keeping a close eye on his friendship with Victoria.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ross returns to the village and attacks Marlon when he realises he implicated him in Shane's murder. Jimmy struggles to cope with the pressure of organising Matthew's funeral.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maria quizzes customers at the salon to see whether they think Tony is capable of murder, and Norris struggles to get rid of Jed, while Molly grows suspicious of Tyrone.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone tells Molly the truth. Audrey decides Maria needs medical help, and Eileen eagerly awaits the arrival of her dad. Meanwhile, Michelle makes Becky feel guilty.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Aidensfield residents are shocked when an MI5 agent turns up in the village showing more than just a passing interest in the activities of Vernon Scripps and his mysterious associate, a man who disembarked from a Russian trawler docked in Whitby harbour. It seems there is an even darker side to the roguish wheeler-dealer than they suspected

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's staff come under suspicion when some African carvings are stolen from the hall - but then an African chieftain arrives claiming the very same pieces belong to his tribe. Vernon sees a business opportunity that needs the royal stamp of approval and probationary PC Tom Nicholson turns up at the station. Nostalgic drama, starring Jason Durr, Rupert Vansittart and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin search for a nursery now that Janice has left, and Mrs Tishell is shocked to find out that Al has very high blood pressure. Louisa also helps with the diagnosis of a pupil, which spurs her on to explore the possibilities of a change of career. This is not welcome news for Martin, who dislikes any kind of change, even if it could be for the better. Elsewhere, Ruth is approached about selling her farm. Starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz, Selina Cadell and Ian McNeice

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP is perturbed when he discovers Louisa, who is already spending much of her time away from home, has applied to take up a child and adolescent counselling course. However, achieving a work-life balance is far from the greatest of concerns for some of the Ellinghams' neighbours, not least of whom is Al, whose plans to demonstrate his business acumen are placed in jeopardy when Ken decides to close the pub. Unless Ruth agrees to serve as a guarantor, Ken will not allow Morwenna and Al to take over the establishment, so the pair arrange a race-walking event to raise funds and prove the community is on their side

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A young man sat alone in the church and covered in blood confesses to Sidney and Geordie that he attacked and killed his landlord. However, when the pair investigate further, they find the alleged victim alive and well with his family. Elsewhere, Margaret's visits to the vicarage begin to leave Mrs Maguire irritated, Leonard is distracted by secretive trips to the cinema, and Geordie is compelled to deal with a surprising shoplifter. Drama set in the 1950s, starring James Norton and Robson Green

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: As the trial of local teenager Gary Bell approaches, Sidney and Geordie find themselves in disagreement regarding his guilt. Meanwhile, a man is convinced that his dead wife is haunting his home and stable yards, and implores Sidney to come to his estate and perform an exorcism for him. The vicar offers prayers and counsel, but when the man is found hanged in the exact same spot in which his wife died, Sidney and Geordie are united by a shared goal as they investigate his suicide. Drama set in the 1950s, starring James Norton and Robson Green

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: First of a two-part story by Lynda La Plante in which the team investigate the death of a prostitute who plunged from the balcony of a block of luxury apartments. They discover she was the daughter of a famous Seventies TV star murdered 12 years previously, whose killer was never traced, and try to find a link between the two crimes. David Hayman stars as the newly promoted DCS Michael Walker, alongside Victoria Smurfit, Colin Salmon and Frances Barber

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Unexplained killings rock the Bodmin community, giving rise to fears that the so-called 'beast', a monster enshrined in local legend, has returned to wreak fresh havoc. Kersey ventures up onto the moors to investigate and does not return - so Wycliffe is forced to go it alone. Jack Shepherd and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: George and Mildred

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a woman is found strangled, a pathologist detects chilling similarities with a number of unsolved murders - prompting Wycliffe and his team to reopen 11-year-old case files. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Tim Wylton and Helen Masters

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team investigate a series of bizarre events in the aftermath of a wedding which boasted a most unusual guest list. It transpires the couple had only met three weeks before the ceremony, and the honeymoon in a luxury hotel ends in a brutal killing. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, with Ronald Hines and Josephine Butler

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Charles investigates the fatal stabbing of a businessman in a pub car park - and gradually discovers the victim was not the upstanding pillar of the community he appeared to be. Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As campaigning for the 1970 general election gets under way in Oxford, racial tensions escalate in the city and a clash between two rival gangs leads Morse and Thursday to the door of a familiar face, where they discover the influence of a right wing organisation hoping to win an Oxford seat. Things get worse at an Indian restaurant, where a mysterious disappearance and a shocking murder put even the strongest family loyalties to the test. Crime drama, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop busies himself organising a christening while awaiting the birth of his first grandchild - and still finds time to cast a beady eye over the contents of an elderly widow's home. Whimsical rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Preparations get under way for the christening, while Primrose loses her heart to a very confused vicar. Pop gets a black eye, Edith Pilchester tackles a gang of roving Teddy boys and Mademoiselle Dupont begins to feel ill. Drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a family glassware business collapses following the death of one of its partners, Barnaby investigates allegations of financial irregularities, love triangles and a string of murders. Lapsed Freemason Jones uses his inside knowledge to explore the ceremonial aspects of two killings, but it is Cully's production of Hamlet that gives Tom the key to the mystery. John Nettles, Jason Hughes, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is delighted that his heroes Hired Gun are reuniting to play at the Midsomer Rocks festival - minus their drummer, who disappeared 30 years ago after driving his car into a quarry. However, a string of bizarre warnings convinces the paranoid lead singer that someone is trying to stop the reunion, and his fears are confirmed when the backing singer drops dead on stage. Radio 2 host Suzi Quatro guest stars

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor Mildred's hopes are raised when work-shy George looks for a job, but his chances of finding gainful employment seem slim. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A blackmailer contaminates a jar of baby food in a local supermarket and promises it will not be the last if he does not receive payment. Wycliffe and his team spring into action, aware they are under investigation by the police disciplinary squad. Crime drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks into a lone yachtswoman's mysterious disappearance. He learns she had been receiving threats from the owners of a local china clay works - where he later makes a grisly discovery. Jack Shepherd stars, with Con O'Neill and Michael Attwell

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin search for a nursery now that Janice has left, and Mrs Tishell is shocked to find out that Al has very high blood pressure. Louisa also helps with the diagnosis of a pupil, which spurs her on to explore the possibilities of a change of career. This is not welcome news for Martin, who dislikes any kind of change, even if it could be for the better. Elsewhere, Ruth is approached about selling her farm. Starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz, Selina Cadell and Ian McNeice

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When director Nick Cheyney is found beheaded by a guillotine on the set of his latest film, Barnaby and Jones are left with no shortage of suspects. The victim, it transpires, was on poor terms with several people in some way connected to the project, including the mother of his child, and an actor who blamed him for ruining his career. Guest starring Nicky Henson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Jeffrey and George get into the festive spirit by challenging each other to a game of computer tennis. Festive instalment of the comedy, from 1978, starring Brian Murphy and Norman Eshley

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper is astonished and thrilled when George buys her an expensive present for their anniversary. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon tells the police that Shane was blackmailing Ross about his affair with Donna, while Val warns Daz that she is keeping a close eye on his friendship with Victoria.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ross returns to the village and attacks Marlon when he realises he implicated him in Shane's murder. Jimmy struggles to cope with the pressure of organising Matthew's funeral.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maria quizzes customers at the salon to see whether they think Tony is capable of murder, and Norris struggles to get rid of Jed, while Molly grows suspicious of Tyrone.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone tells Molly the truth. Audrey decides Maria needs medical help, and Eileen eagerly awaits the arrival of her dad. Meanwhile, Michelle makes Becky feel guilty.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The detective stumbles on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Aidensfield residents are shocked when an MI5 agent turns up in the village showing more than just a passing interest in the activities of Vernon Scripps and his mysterious associate, a man who disembarked from a Russian trawler docked in Whitby harbour. It seems there is an even darker side to the roguish wheeler-dealer than they suspected

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's staff come under suspicion when some African carvings are stolen from the hall - but then an African chieftain arrives claiming the very same pieces belong to his tribe. Vernon sees a business opportunity that needs the royal stamp of approval and probationary PC Tom Nicholson turns up at the station. Nostalgic drama, starring Jason Durr, Rupert Vansittart and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local aristocrats and beekeepers the Deddingtons have built up a thriving empire, producing a honey with extraordinary healing power that has put the village of Granville Norton on the map. But how far will they go to ensure they are never dethroned? Griff Rhys Jones and Imogen Stubbs guest star in the mystery drama, with Neil Dudgeon and Nick Hendrix

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: As the Kings prepare for Matthew's funeral, Jimmy is furious to learn that Tom's gravestone has been defaced. Ross reveals Jasmine visited Shane's flat on the night of the murder.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony and Carla arrive back from honeymoon, oblivious to Maria's suspicions - until they listen to their answering machine. Molly walks out on Tyrone, and Eileen's dad arrives.

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper goes into hospital with suspected appendicitis and George looks forward to some peace and quiet - but Jerry shows up on his doorstep with other ideas. Roy Kinnear guest stars

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George has already denounced the neighbours as toffee-nosed snobs, and events make Mildred wonder whether he could be right. Queenie Watts and Michael Robbins guest star, with Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: As the Kings prepare for Matthew's funeral, Jimmy is furious to learn that Tom's gravestone has been defaced. Ross reveals Jasmine visited Shane's flat on the night of the murder.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony and Carla arrive back from honeymoon, oblivious to Maria's suspicions - until they listen to their answering machine. Molly walks out on Tyrone, and Eileen's dad arrives.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rosie confirms Tony's fears about what Maria knows. Steve's plan to convince Michelle he is a bad boyfriend steps up a gear, and Hayley makes charitable plans for Christmas Day.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack's house burns down, forcing him to move in with a colleague. At work, he becomes involved in the search for a kidnapper after a young woman disappears. Long-running crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Marc Warren

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A spate of petty thefts is traced to a local nursing home, but Blaketon's investigations uncover something far more sinister. Meanwhile, David's first day in his new job goes spectacularly wrong, but Bernie is not surprised. Rural drama, starring Jason Durr and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ashfordly Hall throws open its doors to the public, but all hell breaks loose when a collection of valuable Constable sketches is stolen. Mike and Tom are sent to question the chief suspect, an 80-year-old art thief, only to find him mugged and bound to a chair - with none of the paintings anywhere in sight. Nostalgic drama, starring Jason Durr and Ryan Early

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Anglers flock to the village of Solomon Gorge desperate to catch a giant fish that is said to lurk in the lake. Their peace is ruined however when hundreds of sporting enthusiasts descend on the area for the Psycho Mud Run. Following the bitterness, fishing hooks and barbs are discovered hidden within the race obstacles before someone is found fatally electrocuted. Barnaby is left working out whether his death was another part of a sabotage gone wrong. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jimmy discovers Rodney is holding a hunt at Home Farm and sets out to teach him a lesson. Debbie tries to change Jasmine's mind when she decides to confess to Shane's murder.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chas tells Carl there is no chance of a reconciliation. Rodney offers Jimmy a share of the proceeds from the shoot, while Marlon worries about his living arrangements.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony stops short of confessing to Maria, but leaves the young widow in no doubt about his guilt. Steve consoles the unhappy children's entertainer who he has hired for Amy's party.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony has to stay cool when the police pay him a visit. Gail is shocked by the official switch-on of the Windasses' Christmas lights and Liz introduces a new friend to Michelle.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A prodigal son returns to claim his inheritance, setting off alarm bells for Ventress, whose instinct is confirmed when a series of accidents at the carpet factory signals the management's scant regard for life and limb. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps spies an opportunity to make merry as well as money by organising a beauty pageant, but reckons without the determined opposition of disapproving Joyce Jowett, who will stop at nothing to halt the event

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Hippies colonise Lawson's holiday cottages, causing much local dissent and tut-tutting. The only people who don't object to their presence are an undercover journalist in search of a scoop, and Vernon, who is not slow to spot a way of capitalising on the situation. Starring Geoffrey Hughes and Malcolm Tierney

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a body is dredged up from a slurry pit on a remote farm, the investigation forces owner Danny Pryor to admit he has a number of illegal Serbian workers on the payroll. A faded tattoo identifies the victim as Jack Reeves, a young man from the local travelling community, but what is his connection to the immigrants? As the case unfolds, Vera and her team must connect the dots between Jack and a wounded Serbian, and expose the tragic truth at the heart of Pryor Farm. With Mark Bonnar, Mark Womack and Jim Sturgeon

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Sidney receives a series of anonymous phone calls, which he realises may be linked to a series of murders in which the victims were all important community figures killed by someone who leaves dead birds as a grisly calling card. The threat forces Sidney to question whether he can reconcile his relationship with Amanda and his calling as a vicar. Leonard bonds with Hilary as she cares for her dying father, while Geordie's investigation of the killings leads him to a mysterious home for people with disabilities. Detective drama set in the 1950s, starring James Norton and Robson Green

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Sidney and Geordie join a local cricket team to play a match against nearby Whittlesford, but team captain Geoff Towler (Peter Davison) seems to have an objection to talented young Pakistani batsman Zafar, and is even willing to forfeit the game to spite the player. After the match, a buffet ends badly when contaminated beer leaves everyone feeling ill, a matter Geoff is keen for Geordie to cover up. However, a worse fate awaits Zafar, who is later found to have received a fatal dose of the poison his teammates and spectators had received

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: First of a two-part crime drama by Lynda La Plante. A man vanishes while on his honeymoon in London, leaving his wife Susan distraught. Her father contacts Mike Walker, believing the police are not taking the case seriously, but there is little evidence to suggest foul play. However, rookie detective Lisa West notices a character on CCTV footage who arouses her suspicions. Starring David Hayman, Victoria Smurfit, David Calder and Sarah Ozeke

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team investigate a series of bizarre events in the aftermath of a wedding which boasted a most unusual guest list. It transpires the couple had only met three weeks before the ceremony, and the honeymoon in a luxury hotel ends in a brutal killing. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, with Ronald Hines and Josephine Butler

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's jealousy rears its ugly head, but Mildred is in no hurry to put his mind at rest

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's brother discovers documents that prove Mildred married an Army deserter. Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur goes to the hospital to visit his friend Alex, who has been the unfortunate victim of a hit-and-run incident. Later, Terry is persuaded to mind Alex's antique shop for him, and soon discovers out that not everyone is fond of the retailer. Vintage comedy, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: King of the Roads
programme.desc: The King of the Roads heads to Scandinavia for its latest encounter, the Imatranajo Road Races from Finland.

programme.title: World Rally Championship Highlights
programme.desc: The Rally Estonia. Action from the ninth round of the campaign, based around Tartu. Great Britain's Elfyn Evans currently leads the standings, but Takamoto Katsuta will have been looking to close the gap

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Lisa Kelly takes an oversized trailer to Prudhoe, a task she failed the previous season, while Alex Debogorski picks up a load of fish from Red Sucker Lake and heads for Winnipeg. Rick Yemm is forced to reverse down a treacherously icy road after missing a turning, while in Fairbanks, rookie Maya Seiber is tested on a 3D simulator, which all drivers have to conquer before they can be let loose on testing terrain

programme.desc: Thomas decides to teach a university course on the finer points of being a private investigator, and helps his class delve into a real-life mystery involving a student's boyfriend - who proves to be in big trouble. Tom Selleck stars

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: Thomas agrees to help a 12-year-old half-Asian street merchant find his American father, missing since the war in Vietnam - but his efforts put him into conflict with gangsters and corrupt politicians. Detective drama, starring Tom Selleck

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: An international banker is held to ransom, providing Makepeace with an ideal opportunity to prove a point to her chauvinistic partner as she too gets caught up in the hostage situation. Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber star

programme.desc: Laura and Rocky are asked to follow a man who has recently been released from prison. Meanwhile, Kev is enlisted by a bike shop to track down a bike

programme.desc: Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald meet a passionate car collector and a couple who have been collecting together since their honeymoon.

programme.title: Goodwood Festival of Speed

programme.title: Coogan's Bluff
programme.desc: An Arizona sheriff is sent to New York to bring an imprisoned murderer back to stand trial. Don Siegel's crime drama, starring Clint Eastwood, Lee J Cobb, Don Stroud and Susan Clark

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In the heart of Central America lurks a marine creature believed to reach 8ft in length and weigh up to 300lbs. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade is taken to breaking point as he re-evaluates everything he knows about fishing in an attempt to catch this monster - which is known to leap into boats and cause fatal injuries

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On the Canada-US border lies a vast lake that hides a dark history. For Native Americans it's an ancient and sacred site, but recently something has risen up from the depths to attack swimmers, and stories abound of an invasion of primordial bloodsuckers. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade plunges into the heart of a waterfall in the Pacific Northwest on a mission to catch the oldest river monster of his career

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Maverick British rifleman Richard Sharpe is promoted for saving General Wellesley's life, and sent behind enemy lines to rally Spanish support. Surrounded by the French, he manages to pull off an inspiring propaganda coup in the small town of Torrecastro. Swashbuckling adventure, featuring Downton Abbey creator Julian Fellowes and starring Sean Bean and Brian Cox

programme.desc: After weeks of preparation, Henry Cole participates in an event at Oulton Park, and joins Britain's top drag-bike competitors for a race that lasts a mere seven seconds. He also comes off his motorcycle while trying to master motocross, and this time the injury looks serious
