programme.title: BattleBots
programme.desc: US robot combat series. The BattleBots VII World Championship gets under way with the best combat robots in the world competing for the ultimate prize of the Giant Nut - gunning for a shot at the title are killer Kiwis End Game, the electric HyperShock, the vicious Riptide, undefeated Huge, fan favourites Witch Doctor and many more. This edition sees the first eight robots fight to the death - winners progress to the Round of 16 while the losers go home.

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: The rugged private investigator investigates a case involving his friend Carol as she prepares to testify against a man put behind bars by her father. US detective drama, starring Tom Selleck

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thomas sets out to rescue old friend JJ Quincannon from Cambodia, and Rick bravely tags along to help - but gets shot in an ambush, jeopardising the entire mission. Vintage detective drama, starring Tom Selleck

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A top model is murdered by a hit-and-run driver. Dempsey and Makepeace discover she had connections at the highest level in government circles. When the two detectives make further investigations they uncover some disturbing facts

programme.title: Boon
programme.desc: A fairground big wheel owner helps Ken out on a case as Rocky takes on the case of recovering a trial-winning sheepdog which has been stolen

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The trio race against time to find a Second World War bomb in a bid to stop it falling into the hands of a neo-Nazi group keen to launch another battle. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry arrives home to find a nine-year-old boy on his doorstep claiming to be his son, and ends up with a minding job he had not anticipated. He forms a bond with the youngster, eventually taking his side against his stepfather. Sharon Duce guest stars, with Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: The return of an old enemy leads to a series of problems, beginning with the loss of Sharpe's hard-earned captaincy, and culminating in a race against time to rescue his lover Teresa and their baby from the beleaguered city of Badajoz before Wellesley's forces attack. Military adventure, starring Sean Bean and Pete Postlethwaite

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers: Deadliest Roads
programme.desc: The truckers drive up to a height of 13,000ft in the Himalayas, higher than they have before, as they tackle the Rohtang Pass for the first time. Rick Yemm transports a sacred Buddha statue between monasteries, while Lisa Kelly brings vital supplies to a city normally cut off by snow. With the ice melting and the roads vanishing beneath rivers and gushing waterfalls, they then face their most treacherous route yet

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling - the roster features world-class talent including Jon Moxley and Claudio Castagnoli, and many more who bring a freshness and energy into professional wrestling

programme.title: Dark Side of the Ring
programme.desc: The story of Gino Hernandez, whose family believed that his death was a drug-related murder for 30 years, but startling revelations are exposed to his still-grieving mother

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A professional gambler needs to hide a group of people who might be able to help him win at roulette, so Arthur hits on the idea of using Terry's flat. Comedy drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine and featuring a pre-Birds of a Feather Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan tries to leave Charlie with the restaurant bill during a double date, but his frustrated brother plays him at his own game. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie faces a difficult decision as Berta and Lydia fight over him, and turn on one another when the situation intensifies. Starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: The latest news and reviews from the world of motorsport

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Derby Through the Years
programme.desc: A look back at memorable stagings of the race from Epsom Downs. Featuring brilliant performances from Shergar to Sea the Stars, plus interviews with personalities from the worlds of sport and entertainment

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore presents action from Christmas 1979, featuring Manchester United v Nottingham Forest, Everton v Manchester City and a look back on a decade of the show

programme.title: World Rally Championship Highlights
programme.desc: The Rally Estonia. Action from the ninth round of the campaign, based around Tartu. Great Britain's Elfyn Evans currently leads the standings, but Takamoto Katsuta will have been looking to close the gap

programme.title: E1 Powerboat Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the fourth meeting of the campaign, which came from Monaco

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team are at Ascot ahead of King George Day, which is considered one of the showpiece meetings of the summer season

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Discover the secret recipe of a British kitchen cupboard staple, then see what it takes to become an official scissor putter-togetherer

programme.title: Formula E Live
programme.desc: Race one of the Tokyo E-Prix (Start-time 12.05pm). Coverage of the 14th round of the campaign, held at Tokyo Street Circuit. Pascal Wehrlein of Germany leads the standings, but New Zealand's Mitch Evans is just behind him, with Great Britain's reigning champion Oliver Rowland in third

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole enters his first ever motorbike race and trains with British superbike racer Chris Walker. He then goes off-road riding with Dakar Rally competitor Lyndon Poskitt. Plus, an update on the 1970s Bonneville as Pete Thorne and Henry make their own wheels

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Lisa Kelly takes an oversized trailer to Prudhoe, a task she failed the previous season, while Alex Debogorski picks up a load of fish from Red Sucker Lake and heads for Winnipeg. Rick Yemm is forced to reverse down a treacherously icy road after missing a turning, while in Fairbanks, rookie Maya Seiber is tested on a 3D simulator, which all drivers have to conquer before they can be let loose on testing terrain

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers: Deadliest Roads
programme.desc: The truckers drive up to a height of 13,000ft in the Himalayas, higher than they have before, as they tackle the Rohtang Pass for the first time. Rick Yemm transports a sacred Buddha statue between monasteries, while Lisa Kelly brings vital supplies to a city normally cut off by snow. With the ice melting and the roads vanishing beneath rivers and gushing waterfalls, they then face their most treacherous route yet

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Maverick British rifleman Richard Sharpe is promoted for saving General Wellesley's life, and sent behind enemy lines to rally Spanish support. Surrounded by the French, he manages to pull off an inspiring propaganda coup in the small town of Torrecastro. Swashbuckling adventure, featuring Downton Abbey creator Julian Fellowes and starring Sean Bean and Brian Cox

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood looks back at television sporting firsts, including footage of a 14-year-old Ronnie O'Sullivan who would go on to take snooker by storm

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Discover the secret recipe of a British kitchen cupboard staple, then see what it takes to become an official scissor putter-togetherer

programme.title: Motorsport Mundial
programme.desc: The latest action from the world of motorsport, featuring race coverage, insights, and behind-the-scenes footage from the planet's biggest and best motorsport series

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: The latest news and reviews from the world of motorsport

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Action from early December 1979, featuring Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United, Cambridge United v Queens Park Rangers and Manchester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore presents FA Cup third-round action from January 1980, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United and Halifax Town v Manchester City

programme.title: Goodwood Festival of Speed
programme.desc: Highlights of the motoring event, featuring the likes of Lando Norris, Kimi Antonelli, Damon Hill, Valentino Rossi and Gerhard Berger, plus the Hill Climb shoot-out and celebrations of 100 years of Ducati

programme.title: Live British Touring Car Championship
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents coverage of the 13th, 14th and 15th races of the season, which take place at Thruxton Circuit in Hampshire. At the halfway point in the season Napa Racing UK driver Ash Sutton comes into this event at the top of the standings, 48 points ahead of rival Tom Ingram who is in second

programme.title: The Best of the 80s
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of sports covered by ITV during the 1980s, ranging from football and boxing to wrestling and double decker racing

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: The rugged private investigator investigates a case involving his friend Carol as she prepares to testify against a man put behind bars by her father. US detective drama, starring Tom Selleck

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thomas sets out to rescue old friend JJ Quincannon from Cambodia, and Rick bravely tags along to help - but gets shot in an ambush, jeopardising the entire mission. Vintage detective drama, starring Tom Selleck

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A top model is murdered by a hit-and-run driver. Dempsey and Makepeace discover she had connections at the highest level in government circles. When the two detectives make further investigations they uncover some disturbing facts

programme.title: Boon
programme.desc: A fairground big wheel owner helps Ken out on a case as Rocky takes on the case of recovering a trial-winning sheepdog which has been stolen

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The trio race against time to find a Second World War bomb in a bid to stop it falling into the hands of a neo-Nazi group keen to launch another battle. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Part one of two. First episode of the influential fantasy series, starring Michael Praed as Robin of Loxley, a simple villager who finds himself cast in the role of saviour of the common people. With Anthony Valentine and Nickolas Grace

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: Part two of two. Rick falls for a Vietnamese girl and considers taking her back to America with him, while Thomas's friend Tyler bravely sacrifices himself to save the lives of two children. Vintage detective drama, starring Tom Selleck

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: Thomas tangles with Yakuza hitmen while trying to find the crook who sold Rick a boat stolen from a rental company in Long Beach, and Higgins teaches three Japanese women about the merits of life in America. Tom Selleck stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo investigate the killings of a drug dealer and an SI-10 officer and are soon on the trail of an assassin hired by a powerful American crimelord. Drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Boon
programme.desc: Ken is in for a few surprises when he investigates a woman whose husband suspects her of committing adultery

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The discovery of wartime Japanese armaments alerts the Nemesis agents to the activities of a gun-running cartel with a sideline in murder

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: Part two of two. Rick falls for a Vietnamese girl and considers taking her back to America with him, while Thomas's friend Tyler bravely sacrifices himself to save the lives of two children. Vintage detective drama, starring Tom Selleck

programme.desc: Thomas tangles with Yakuza hitmen while trying to find the crook who sold Rick a boat stolen from a rental company in Long Beach, and Higgins teaches three Japanese women about the merits of life in America. Tom Selleck stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo investigate the killings of a drug dealer and an SI-10 officer and are soon on the trail of an assassin hired by a powerful American crimelord. Drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Boon
programme.desc: Ken is in for a few surprises when he investigates a woman whose husband suspects her of committing adultery

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The discovery of wartime Japanese armaments alerts the Nemesis agents to the activities of a gun-running cartel with a sideline in murder

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Conclusion. Robin and his outlaw band storm the evil Baron de Belleme's stronghold in a bid to rescue Lady Marion and retrieve the silver arrow. Michael Praed, Anthony Valentine and Judi Trott star

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: The Hawaii-based private detective is hired to find the sheet music to five lost songs allegedly written by George Lee Jessup, a singer who died in 1954 - but finds their true authorship is shrouded in mystery. Tom Selleck stars

programme.title: Magnum, PI
programme.desc: The ever-chivalrous Thomas steps in to help a distraught civil servant search for her husband, who has mysteriously done a vanishing act. Detective drama, starring Tom Selleck

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo probe a colleague's death, which brings to light the officer's investigations into the activities of right-wing extremists

programme.title: Boon
programme.desc: A dairy owner mistrusts his wife and hires Ken to keep an eye on her. Harry, meanwhile, finds himself looking down the wrong end of a shotgun

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: Two members of the team come to blows when Nemesis agents find themselves programmed to kill their own colleagues. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon as crusading heroes with superhuman powers

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur celebrates a reunion with old army pal Yorkie. However, after a particularly hard drinking session the visitor loses both his trousers and his bearings, leaving it up to Terry to track him down before his wife is made aware of the situation. Brian Glover guest stars, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Formula E Highlights
programme.desc: The Tokyo ePrix. Action from the 14th and 15th rounds of the season, which took place at Tokyo Street Circuit. Germany's Pascal Wehrlein came into this event at the top of the drivers' championship and held a nine point lead over New Zealand's Mitch Evans in second

programme.title: King of the Roads
programme.desc: Action from the Skerries 100, a 100-mile road race in Ireland that attracts some of the world's best riders

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Hugh Rowland and Rick Yemm take part in a convoy carrying one of the biggest loads ever to cross the Canadian ice as they transport three prefabricated sections of a new airport building to St Theresa Point in northern Manitoba. Having graduated from the simulator, rookie driver Maya Sieber is about to take on the ice road for the first time with veteran Phil Krom in the passenger seat. How will she fare?

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Collision
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action, featuring stars including FTR, Thunder Rosa, Jon Moxley and Jay White. With high-flying, hard-hitting, over-the-top action from some of the best wrestlers on the planet. Tony Schiavone and Nigel McGuinness present

programme.title: Dark Side of the Ring
programme.desc: The legacy of one of wrestling's first female stars, The Fabulous Moolah, is reexamined after controversial allegations surface following her death

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry minds a boxer, but is less than impressed to discover the promoter is betting on his opponent to win the next bout. Arthur, meanwhile, switches bets. Alfred Marks, Paul Barber and Vicki Michelle guest star

programme.title: BattleBots
programme.desc: US robot combat series. The BattleBots VII World Championship gets under way with the best combat robots in the world competing for the ultimate prize of the Giant Nut - gunning for a shot at the title are killer Kiwis End Game, the electric HyperShock, the vicious Riptide, undefeated Huge, fan favourites Witch Doctor and many more. This edition sees the first eight robots fight to the death - winners progress to the Round of 16 while the losers go home.

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Sunrisers Leeds Women v Southern Brave Women (Start-time 11.00am). Coverage of the match at Headingley Cricket Ground. The Sunrisers, who were known as the Northern Superchargers last year won the competition in 2025 after they defeated the Brave by seven wickets in the final. The visitors had finished top of the table 12 months ago but the hosts dominated the final as they chased down their target of 116 with 12 balls to spare

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Sunrisers Leeds Men v Southern Brave Men (Start-time 2.30pm). Coverage of the match at Headingley Cricket Ground. The hosts won this meeting last season thanks to a three-wicket victory in Southampton. The Brave hit 139-5 in their 100 balls before the hosts, known then as the Northern Superchargers, won the game off the very last ball of the contest as Graham Clark led the way with 38 not out from 24 balls

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Southern Brave Women v MI London Women (Start-time 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from Utilita Bowl, Southampton. The hosts won this meeting last year thanks to a resounding 89-run victory on this ground. The Brave made 161-6 in their 100 balls with South Africa's Laura Wolvaardt top-scoring with 36 from 19 balls. In reply the visitors were bowled out for just 72 with Mady Villiers taking 3-17 in her 20 balls

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Sunrisers Leeds Men v Manchester Super Giants Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from Headingley, Leeds, as the local rivals go head to head. The Super Giants, who were known as the Manchester Originals last season, came out on top in the two meetings between the two teams in 2025. They won their home game by 57 runs thanks to an impressive all-round performance that saw them bowl out Leeds for just 114. They then won the game at this venue by seven wickets, chasing down their target of 140 with 16 balls to spare

programme.title: Live Formula 1
programme.desc: The Hungarian Grand Prix (Start-time 2.00pm). Coverage of the 11th round of the season at Hungaroring in Mogyoród. McLaren-Mercedez have won the last two Grand Prix's here with Oscar Piastri winning in 2024 and Lando Norris coming out on top last year. Ferrari's Lewis Hamilton holds the record for the most victories at this event with eight wins between 2007 and 2020, four more than F1 legend Michael Schumacher

programme.title: EFL Cup Retro
programme.desc: Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur in the 2008/09 final. Spurs were managed by Harry Redknapp and had a strong run that led them to the final of the competition, having beaten the likes of Newcastle, Liverpool and Watford in previous rounds. The Red Devils had beaten Middlesbrough, Queens Park Rangers and Blackburn Rovers in the early rounds, before beating Derby County 4-3 on aggregate

programme.title: Live Ladies European Tour Golf
programme.desc: The ISPS HANDA Women's Scottish Open. Coverage of the first day of the tournament, which takes place at Dundonald Links in Irvine, Ayrshire. This is the sixth consecutive year that this event has taken place at this venue, as England's Lottie Woad looks to defend the title that she won here last year. She picked up her first LPGA Tour victory 12 months ago when she finished three strokes clear of South Korea's Kim Hyo-joo

programme.title: Live European Seniors Tour Golf
programme.desc: The Senior Open Championship. Coverage of day one of the fifth Major tournament of the year, which takes place at Gleneagles in Auchterarder, Perthshire. Ireland's Pádraig Harrington is the reigning champion following his triumph at Sunningdale Golf Club in Berkshire 12 months ago, where he finished on an overall score of 16-under-par, three strokes ahead of Thomas Bjørn and Justin Leonard who tied for second place

programme.title: Live PGA Tour Golf
programme.desc: The 3M Open. Further coverage of day one of the tournament, which takes place at TPC Twin Cities in Blaine, Minnesota. This is a relatively recent addition to the PGA circuit with the event first taking place in 2019. Six out of the seven winners of this tournament have come from the United States, with Kurt Kitayama winning here 12 months ago. The American ended on 23-under-par as he finished one stroke clear of nearest challenger Sam Stevens

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: MI London Men v Sunrisers Leeds Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London. The hosts, who were known as Oval Invincibles last season, won the final last year thanks to an impressive 26-run victory over Trent Rockets. They made 168-5 in their 100 balls with Will Jacks top-scoring with 72 from just 41 balls, before they restricted the Rockets to 142-8 in their reply

programme.desc: The Hungarian Grand Prix (Start-time 2.00pm). Coverage of the 11th round of the season at Hungaroring in Mogyoród. McLaren-Mercedez have won the last two Grand Prix's here with Oscar Piastri winning in 2024 and Lando Norris coming out on top last year. Ferrari's Lewis Hamilton holds the record for the most victories at this event with eight wins between 2007 and 2020, four more than F1 legend Michael Schumacher

programme.title: Live Formula 1
programme.desc: Reaction and analysis from the Hungarian Grand Prix

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Southern Brave Women v MI London Women (Start-time 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from Utilita Bowl, Southampton. The hosts won this meeting last year thanks to a resounding 89-run victory on this ground. The Brave made 161-6 in their 100 balls with South Africa's Laura Wolvaardt top-scoring with 36 from 19 balls. In reply the visitors were bowled out for just 72 with Mady Villiers taking 3-17 in her 20 balls

programme.desc: Sunrisers Leeds Women v Manchester Super Giants Women (Start-time 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from Headingley, Leeds

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Sunrisers Leeds Men v Manchester Super Giants Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from Headingley, Leeds, as the local rivals go head to head. The Super Giants, who were known as the Manchester Originals last season, came out on top in the two meetings between the two teams in 2025. They won their home game by 57 runs thanks to an impressive all-round performance that saw them bowl out Leeds for just 114. They then won the game at this venue by seven wickets, chasing down their target of 140 with 16 balls to spare

programme.title: Live Tennis
programme.desc: The Washington DC Open. Coverage of day two of the WTA 500 and ATP 500 event, a hard-court tournament held at William H.G. FitzGerald Tennis Center in the USA. Americans McCartney Kessler and Caroline Dolehide were involved in the only match to require a third set in the opening round of the women's draw last year, and it was the latter who prevailed 7-6 5-7 6-3. Emma Raducanu and Katie Boulter were the only British entrants and experienced contrasting fortunes, with Raducanu booking her place in round two with 7-6 6-4 win over Marta Kostyuk, while Boulter suffered an early exit after losing 3-6 4-6 to Maria Sakkari

programme.desc: MI London Men v Sunrisers Leeds Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London. programme.desc: Southern Brave Men v Welsh Fire Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from Utilita Bowl in Southampton. programme.title: Live Tennis
programme.desc: The Washington DC Open. Coverage of day one of the WTA 500 and ATP 500 event, a hard-court tournament held at William H.G. FitzGerald Tennis Center in the USA. Cameron Norrie came out on top in an all-British contest against Billy Harris in the first round of last year's competition, with a 6-3, 7-6 victory seeing him progress to the second round. His compatriot Dan Evans gained a wild card entry to the event and made the most of it as he booked his place in round two with 3-6 6-4 6-3 win over Belgium's Zizou Bergs

programme.title: Live Tennis
programme.desc: The Washington DC Open. Coverage of day two of the WTA 500 and ATP 500 event, a hard-court tournament held at William H.G. FitzGerald Tennis Center in the USA. Americans McCartney Kessler and Caroline Dolehide were involved in the only match to require a third set in the opening round of the women's draw last year, and it was the latter who prevailed 7-6 5-7 6-3. Emma Raducanu and Katie Boulter were the only British entrants and experienced contrasting fortunes, with Raducanu booking her place in round two with 7-6 6-4 win over Marta Kostyuk, while Boulter suffered an early exit after losing 3-6 4-6 to Maria Sakkari

programme.title: Live: One Day International Cricket
programme.desc: West Indies v New Zealand. Coverage of the fifth and final ODI of the series from Kensington Oval in Barbados. The hosts dominated the last five-match series against the Kiwis back in 2012 when they came out on top 4-1. They won the first two games by nine wickets and then 55 runs before the visitors fought back to claim the third game by 88 runs. It didn't get any better after that for New Zealand with the West Indies then winning the last two matches by 24 and 20 runs respectively

programme.title: Live: Commonwealth Games 360
programme.desc: Coverage from the day session on day one of the Games, which take place in Glasgow. The games first took place back in Hamilton, Canada in 1930 and took place again in 1934 and 1938, before being cancelled in 1942 and 1946 because of the Second World War. Since its return in 1950 the games have continued to take place every four years and are being held in Glasgow for the second time in 12 years, having previously been held in the Scottish city in 2014. Events taking place today include swimming and para swimming, boxing, para powerlifting and artistic gymnastics

programme.desc: Coverage from the night session on day one of the Games, which take place in Glasgow, with medals up for grabs in Para Powerlifting, Swimming, and Para Swimming

programme.title: Live: Commonwealth Games 360
programme.desc: Coverage from the day session on day two of the Games, which take place in Glasgow. The Scottish city previously hosted these games back in 2014, with other British cities to host include Manchester, Edinburgh, Cardiff and London. Birmingham hosted the previous event in 2022 which saw a record-breaking 5054 competitors take part. Events taking place today include netball, boxing, artistic gymnastics, and bowls and para bowls

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary following teams of detectives and specialists involved in murder investigations. In the early hours of the morning in Droitwich Spa, Worcestershire, a 999 call is made by Mohamed Samak saying he has found his wife Jo in their home with a knife in her stomach. Despite attempts to resuscitate her, Jo is declared dead at the scene and the officers arrest the husband on suspicion of murder. But as the investigation continues, the overall picture is more suggestive of suicide rather than homicide

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: Police officers in Bermuda - including around 30 from the UK - deal with the highs and lows of policing this British Overseas Territory. Bermuda's natural beauty is renowned around the world, and nowhere illustrates this more than Cooper's Island Nature Reserve on the south-east tip of the island. Cameras record officers searching for lawbreakers in an ecological nature reserve and the coastguard conducting scuba-diving exercises that include recovering a fake dead body. Narrated by Shyko Amos

programme.title: I Am Legend
programme.desc: A Manhattan-based military scientist finds himself immune to a virus that turns humans into ferocious vampire-like creatures. After the disease has swept across the world, wiping out an estimated 90 per cent of the human population, he devotes his solitary existence to finding a cure by unlocking the secret within his blood. Sci-fi thriller, starring Will Smith, Alice Braga and Charlie Tahan

programme.title: Alan Carr's Picture Slam
programme.desc: Alan Carr hosts this game show in which three teams of contestants have to identify a board full of images, with a chance of winning up to £10,000 if they know them all. This week's contestants are friends Selin and Harin from London, cousins Gerard and Jeremy from South Wales and Kev and Bex, a couple from Motherwell

programme.desc: When a celebrity weather reporter is found dead, Lily investigates if his zero-star reviews of all the local businesses have driven one of their own to murder. Crime drama, starring Sally Phillips and Charlotte Jordan

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick travels north along his favourite coastline as he recalls the catastrophic bushfires of the black summer of 2019 which ravaged this area of the South Coast

Owners Paula and Neil desperately need to reclaim space as their family is about to expand. However, the gang lose track of time during a cuckoo clock fix

programme.title: Hunting the Giant Bluefin Tuna: Our Lives
programme.desc: Atlantic bluefin tuna have returned to Northern Ireland's coast, inspiring Ballycastle anglers Davy Frayne and Jonny Thursby to establish a new fishery. Battling dangerous seas, strong currents and scepticism, they pursue giant catches while hoping to revive the town's proud fishing heritage with an ambitious new industry

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Gracie and husband Lance hope to fund specialist physiotherapy for daughter Juno, who has a rare condition affecting her development, by auctioning family treasures. Auctioneer Izzie Balmer uncovers valuable finds while restorer Robin Johnson revives neglected items, including a rocking horse, before an emotional auction raises money for Juno's future

programme.title: Granite Harbour
programme.desc: A missing persons case turns into a double murder investigation and age-old tensions between the traveller and traditional Aberdonian communities threaten to explode. Davis and the team call on the help of an experienced liaison officer to find a way into the travelling community as understanding it quickly is pivotal to finding the truth. Return of the crime drama, starring Romario Simpson and Hannah Donaldson

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Nabil Abdulrashid and Lucy Beaumont, broadcaster Gethin Jones and actress Francesca Mills join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the offbeat panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Ann Droid
programme.desc: Sue offers to work at the charity shop for the day, but it's a disaster, thanks to Linda, and when Sue tries to show up for Michael at Renee's BJJ presentation, things get even worse. Comedy, starring Diane Morgan and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Austin
programme.desc: Austin's rock star persona spirals, Mel faces scrutiny over her sex life, and Ingrid discovers who is secretly writing Big Bear. Comedy, starring Ben Miller and Sally Phillips

programme.title: Austin
programme.desc: After appearing on a controversial podcast and getting Haley fired, Austin faces a family intervention. Meanwhile, Ingrid is auditioning the whole of Canberra to find an actor to play Big Bear before she loses the show. The last audition, a late entry, may be the one. Comedy, starring Ben Miller and Sally Phillips

programme.title: La La Land
programme.desc: Jobbing actress Mia and struggling jazz pianist Sebastian fall in love and attempt to realise their respective dreams in Los Angeles - a city known for crushing hopes and breaking hearts. However, keeping the wolf from the door without compromising their beliefs proves harder than expected. Damien Chazelle's Bafta-winning musical, starring Emma Stone, Ryan Gosling and John Legend

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Anna boards the train to the north coast along one of the most spectacular railway journeys on the island to meet Derry Girls actor Ian McElhinney. In the seaside town of Portrush, Ian reminisces about childhood holidays spent enjoying dulse, a local seaweed snack. Inspired, Anna heads to the shoreline at low tide to harvest some for a delicious recipe. After that the pair move inland to try pig farming and produce cured meats reminiscent of the Mediterranean

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned, as he visits Cornwall, Owain Wyn Evans heads to Birmingham and Tommy Walsh lands in Gillingham, Kent. After three months the team returns to review the progress of the buyers

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Berkshire need Jules Hudson's help to find a new home in Suffolk with holiday let potential - but they must decide if the business or the home takes precedence

programme.title: Night at the Museum
programme.desc: A new nightwatchman at New York's Museum of Natural History discovers his job entails more than he bargained for. Once night falls, an ancient Egyptian spell brings the museum exhibits to life, entangling him in a riotous struggle to restore peace and order before the sun rises and visitors arrive. Fantasy comedy, starring Ben Stiller, Dick Van Dyke, Robin Williams, Carla Gugino and Ricky Gervais

programme.title: Prayer and Reflection
programme.desc: The musical Friday evening service from Nottingham Liberal Synagogue, which is part of the Progressive Judaism movement. The inclusive service, led by Rabbi Gili Zidkiyahu alongside co-Rabbi Josh Levy, welcomes all members of the community together to usher in the Sabbath

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road Through North Wales
programme.desc: Seven celebrities of differing faiths and beliefs experience an emotional journey on a pilgrimage in north Wales, a route linked by churches dedicated to Celtic early Christian saints. Eshaan Akbar, Amanda Lovett, Spencer Matthews, Christine McGuinness, Tom Rosenthal, Sonali Shah and Michaela Strachan begin their adventure at Flint Castle on the bank of the Dee Estuary

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Buckinghamshire, Sarah Moore scours Beaconsfield Recycling Centre for items that can be given a new lease of life rather than hitting the skips. A retro orange armchair is a task for upholsterer Leanne Watts, while upcycling siblings Katie Stone and Sophie Lawrence want to add a 'Sixties Groove' to a tallboy and dresser set. For her own project, Sarah has a vision for some discarded spectacle cases

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts from Long Marston in Warwickshire, with experts Charles Hanson and Nick Hall helping two teams to maximise their earnings at auction. Eric also learns about the green credentials of antiques

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Laura Wright visits former Catholic Seminary Ushaw Historic House in County Durham, to explore the deep spiritual meaning of trees in the Bible and in Christian faith. Laura also visits Perthshire in Scotland, the home of an ancient tree believed to be thousands of years old, which attracts visitors from all over the world

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Eddie Marsan presents this appeal on behalf of Tourettes Action, a charity which provides support, information and advocacy to improve the lives of people living with Tourette's syndrome

programme.title: The Mating Game
programme.desc: Footage of mating rituals for aquatic creatures, including the lengthy journeys of humpback whales, where a female leads up to 20 males in a spectacular chase that tests their stamina and determination. The programme also examines coral reefs, which are home to clown fish, flatworms and nudibranches. However, some fish are forced to take their mating rituals away from their territory, putting them at risk from reef mantas - who after feasting, begin their own courtship dances

programme.title: Dolittle
programme.desc: In Victorian era Britain, Dr John Dolittle is a Welsh vet with the ability to communicate with animals. After his wife, Lily, dies at sea, he retreats from society and only tends to animals. However, he is eventually drawn into a mission to save the Queen's life. Fantasy adventure, starring Robert Downey Jr, Antonio Banderas and Michael Sheen, while Emma Thompson, Rami Malek and Tom Holland lend their voices to some of the creatures

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin gets flashbacks to his childhood in a mid-terrace house in Coventry and Martel Maxwell visits a former chip shop in Cumbria. Martin Roberts is in the Welsh Valleys

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle, Megan McCubbin, Hamza Yassin and Kate Humble go behind the scenes at Longleat House and Safari Park for another summer. The keepers race to protect aging lioness Malaika before the pride turns on her, and Ben attempts to outsmart the macaques before they destroy their new playground

programme.title: Trace, Track, Get My Car Back
programme.desc: Documentary following teams of investigators who employ trackers using hi-tech methods to hunt down stolen vehicles. Tracker Neil and his team are closing in on a Land Rover linked to a spate of street robberies in Manchester. With the car in their sights, they call for police back-up

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: When fire crews attended a blaze at a hostel in Sussex, they quickly discovered a woman's burned body, apparently having been initially subjected to a brutal attack. Police needed to know what had happened in the victim's final moments, so they called on a blood-spatter analyst. Because of fire damage, carrying out a forensic investigation of the crime scene was going to be extremely challenging so Jo Millington used an innovative technique to remove the soot and uncover the truth. Also in this edition, how a cold case was solved using familial DNA for the first time

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy Walsh struggles for headroom in mid-terrace house in Essex and Jacqui Joseph watches her step in a ground floor flat in south London. Owain Wyn Evans visits a property with huge potential in south Wales

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams compete at Ardingly antiques fair, aided by Jonathan Pratt and Kate Bliss. In East Grinstead, Charlie Ross learns about pioneering treatments for Second World War airmen

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Outdoor adventures are calling in Cumbria, where Jules Hudson helps a couple on the hunt for a rural home with potential for a workshop. After years of success on the Paralympic cycling stage, retirement from sport means time to launch a carpentry business, embrace their shared love of the great outdoors, and live life by and in the water. Jules lines up some tempting homes, including a mystery house with plenty of space for both work and play

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: One of Scotland's most famous figures comes to light, a polished yet practical piece is up for sale, and the dealers are moved by a family story of wartime courage

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The park prepares for the return of hippos after more than 50 years without any. As the team race to finish a state-of-the-art hippo house, the keepers travel across Europe on a mission to learn everything they can about their new arrivals but bringing together a pod of hippos who've never met and mixing them with the park's sea lions could spell disaster

programme.title: Trace, Track, Get My Car Back
programme.desc: The work of trackers using hi-tech methods to hunt down stolen vehicles. Richard races to find a stolen Audi before it's dismantled. According to the vehicle's tracker, it's being stored in a garage in Newport, south Wales. At Felixstowe port, stolen vehicle examiner Adam discovers a shipping container crammed full of stolen car parts. His job is to piece together the parts and identify the stolen vehicles, one by one

programme.title: Expert Witness

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewers tackle the fast fashion industry in reduce, reuse and recycle week, with three challenges focused on the environment and sustainability. They begin by making three pairs of knickers from old T-shirts - a task that tests both their technical skills and their consistency. They then use various waste items, from baubles to bike tyres, to help decorate their transformations, before creating made-to-measure outfits with an environmental message

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: How five men were convicted of the murder of 23-year-old Aurman Singh, a DPD driver who was ambushed and killed in Shrewsbury. Detectives used CCTV, phone records and ANPR data to trace the vehicle in which the men fled the scene and managed to track them to Austria

programme.title: Blinded by the Light
programme.desc: Javed Khan is a British-Pakistani college student living in 1980s Bedfordshire under a domineering father. Inspired by the rock star and a teacher who encourages his own writing, he sets out to fulfill his dreams. Coming-of-age drama, starring Viveik Kalra, Kulvinder Ghir and Meera Ganatra
